Article.ArticleTitle: Chronic Hand Eczema, Real World, and Patient Centricity: A Narrative Review.

Abstract.AbstractText: Chronic hand eczema is a multifactorial, fluctuating, inflammatory skin disease of the hands and wrists, defined as hand eczema that persists for at least 3 months or recurs at least twice within 1 year. Chronic hand eczema is heterogeneous in nature with different clinical manifestations. This chronic condition can significantly impact daily activities and health-related quality of life for patients, including notable physical, psychosocial, occupational, and socioeconomic burdens. However, awareness of the disease and the patient experience remains poor. This review aims to improve understanding of chronic hand eczema in the context of its underlying pathogenesis, clinical subtypes and presentation, and risk factors. Additionally, an overview of the patient experience of the disease, including health-related quality of life and socioeconomic implications, is provided. Improved understanding of chronic hand eczema will support optimal patient care by empowering healthcare providers to more quickly and correctly diagnose this disease with high unmet treatment needs and subsequently offer appropriate holistic care.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Division of Dermatology, Queen's University, Kingston, Canada; Department of Dermatology, Venerology and Allergy, Charité-Universitätsmedizin Berlin, Berlin, Germany. sonja-christine.molin@charite.de.
Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai, New York, USA.
LEO Pharma A/S, Ballerup, Denmark and Department of Dermatology, Bispebjerg Hospital, University of Copenhagen, Denmark.
Barts Health NHS Trust & QMUL, London, United Kingdom.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: SM has received honoraria as consultant/adviser or speaker and/or grants from AbbVie, Almirall, Aralez, Arcutis, Basilea, Bausch Health and Lomb, Bristol Myer Squibb, Boehringer Ingelheim, Evidera, Galderma, GSK, Incyte, Jamp Biopharma, LEO Pharma A/S, Eli Lilly, Novartis, Pfizer, Sanofi, Sun Pharma, and UCB. She is currently an investigator for Novartis and LEO Pharma A/S. EG-Y is an employee of Mount Sinai and has received research grants (paid to the institution) from Abbvie, Amgen, AnaptysBio, Arcutis, Aslan, Bristol Myers Squibb, Concert, Eli Lilly, GSK, Incyte, Inmagene Bio, Janssen, LEO Pharma A/S, Pfizer, Q32 Bio, Regeneron, and Sanofi. She is a consultant for Abbvie, Almirall, Amgen, AnaptysBio, Apogee Therapeutics, Apollo Therapeutics, Artax Biopharma, Astria, Bristol Myers Squibb, Boehringer Ingelheim, Calliditas, Cara Therapeutics, Celldex, Centrexion Therapeutics, Connect Bio-pharm, Coty, DBV Technologies, Eli Lilly, Enveda Biosciences, Escient Pharmaceuticals, Fairmount Funds Management, FL2022-001, Inc., Galderma, Gate Bio, Google Ventures, GSK Immunology, Incyte, Inmagene, Janssen Biotech, Jasper Therapeutics, Kymera Therapeutics, Kyowa Kirin, LEO Pharma A/S, Matchpoint Therapeutics, Merck, Nektar Therapeutics, Novartis Pharmaceuticals, NUMAB Therapeutics AG, Nuvig, OrbiMed Advisors, OTSUKA, Pfizer, Pharmaxis, Pioneering Medicines, Proteologix US, Inc., RAPT, RayThera, Inc., Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Ribon Therapeutics, Sanofi, SATO, Schrödinger, Sitryx, Sun Pharma Advanced Research Company (SPARC), Takeda, Teva Branded Pharmaceutical Products R&D, Inc., UCB, and Ventyx Biosciences. JPT is an employee and stockholder of LEO Pharma A/S and has been a speaker/investigator/adviser for AbbVie, Almirall, Arena Pharmaceuticals, Aslan Pharmaceuticals, Eli Lilly, LEO Pharma A/S, OM Pharma, Pfizer, Regeneron, Sanofi-Genzyme, and Union Therapeutics. AB has had ad hoc consultancy/travel bursaries/lecturing fees from AbbVie, Almirall, Bristol Myers Squibb, Galderma, Janssen Pharmaceuticals, LEO Pharma A/S, Lilly, MSD, Novartis Pfizer, Sanofi, and UCB.
